VerbalDescription: Take the piece that the most connected (biggest) and make a grid size that will only fit that piece.  Then copy that piece (including the same color) into the grid.
grid 5 x 7, draw the t shape from the image

This might be interpreted as one object broken into several pieces. Create a grid corresponding to the width and height of the largest visible piece, and then recreate that piece within, as though you were zooming in on that piece. Ignore all other pieces if present.

Find your largest or longest object. You want to create the new grid to fit that object and only that object. Think of like you are zooming in on that specific object. So, if your object is 7 wide and 5 long, make your grid that size and color in the same object.
resize the grid to the dimensions of the largest connected shape which is the t shape. keep the colour the same and copy the shape into the resized grid

Find the largest shape in the demonstration area. The dimensions of that shape become the dimensions for the grid, so that the shape fills the grid from edge to edge. The shape and color remain the same as in the demonstration input.
Find the largest shape and then make it fit the entire frame. Keep the same color.
Find the biggest shape. Make the grid the same size as that shape. Match the colors of that shape.
The grid was resized to only accept the main pattern.
Resize the grid to fit the entire shape of the main shape. Make sure to keep the existing pattern of the shape and remove any excess small shapes on the grid.
The rule for this task is to select the largest image, and base the grid size based on that object and mimic that size and shape.
Here the largest object becomes the focal point. Make your grid the same height and width as your largest object. Then fill this grid with that shape in its corresponding colour.
whatever the biggest clump of boxes is that are the same color, you zoom in the input box to as small as it can go with it fitting inside it
Grid size will be the size of the largest object. Copy largest object (keep its color the same).

Identify in the input the object made up of multiple connected squares of the same color. Create an output grid just large enough to encompass the input object. copy the input object into that grid and color to match.
Delete everything but the largest group of pixels and the background color blocks as wide or long as that pixel immediately bordering it
Find the smaller orange shape that is repeated within the grid. Resize the grid to recreate just that single shape.
grid 5 x 5, fill with orange, then make the center square yellow
Find a repeating pattern of smaller multicolored grids within the larger grid. Make a grid the same size as one of those patterns and fill it with the same colors.
Copy small shape from task. Grid is same size as shape, no empty black background squares.
Identify which one of the two types of pattern has two colours. Resize to the dimensions of one of those sections of that pattern. Copy the colours as they are.
Identfiy box containing two colours (there may be mutiple just select one) and set grid size to the same dimensions. Recreate the box using same colours and same positions.
Make a grid that is just one of the shapes shown. No extra squares other than the shape. Same colours
Copy the two-colored shape from the pattern into a grid of that shape's dimensions and keep the original colors.
square boxes are correct
Take the shape with squares in it. This becomes the final shape, filling the entire dimension.

take a single shape (one colored rectangle, containing a smaller differently colored rectangle)from the array or pattern of matching shapes and turn the rest of the grid black. resize the grid to fit the single remaining shape.
Change the grid size to the size of the colourful shape within the test grid (3x3). Then copy the shape into the space
The colourful segment in occupies the new grid entirely and grid is the size of the segment
It is not the same colour box. 3X3 and will have two colours. It is a pattern
Pick the shape that has more than 1 color and a pattern, the shape should also be repeated, make the grid the same size as that shape and copy the exact shape and color into the grid
Make your grid 3x3. Copy the pattern that has 2 colors. Disregard the solid color surrounding it.
Replicate the unique shape seen in the input. Grid size and shading to be the same as that of the unique shape seen in the input

You will be presented with a grid that will have shape comprised of two separate colours eg. a square with red and green. It does not matter how many of these you can see. Your solution is to replicate just one of these objects exactly. Therefore you need to make a grid that is exactly the same size as the object you are copying. If you have a rectangle of green and red that is four squares high, and six squares wide, that will be the size of your grid. You can then colour in the boxes to exactly match the image in your test example.

You will be presented with an image that will have a repeating shape comprised of two colours. It does not matter how many times that image occurs. You will copy just one example of it. So, if the image is a repeating rectangle of four high and five wide grid, then this is what you will make. To reiterate, the grid you make will be the same size exactly as the image you will copy. Note, the image to be copied may be interspersed with blank squares or coloured squares. Just ensure you use the image of the object that is repeated, and made of squares using two colours.
Locate a repeating shape with two colors in the input. Make the new grid size the same size of this shape. Draw this single object on the grid.
Copy the size and shape of the two colors that are in the minority in the input.

Take a single part of the pattern that is a combination of two rectangles of different colors combined to make one rectangle. Shrink the grid size to the dimensions of the pattern element and fill it with the shape and color of that element.

There will be a shape constructed of two colors that repeats over a solid background. Resize your new grid to the size of the two-color shape and copy one over exactly. Disregard all other copies and the solid background.

Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one object in the input with two colours, in your answer, eliminate the one with the  and adjust the height and width of the grid with different colour ( keep its colour the same).

Identify the shape within the original grid that stands out from the pattern. Size the new grid to the tightest dimensions possible to copy over that shape. Copy the shape into the new grid with the same color, disregarding any of the pattern.
Take the shape that is imposed onto the background pattern and simply use that shape as the solution.
Resize the grid so it encompasses the edges of the different coloured pattern. Colour that pattern as in the original. Leave the other cells black.
look for the object distinct from the pattern and size the grid to this objects dimensions and then exactly reproduce the object colour and size

There is a regular background pattern.  Ignore this.  Create a black grid big enough to contain the shape outlined against the regular pattern with no black blocks surrounding it.  Copy the shape in the same colour 1:1 scale.  Leave any surrounding blocks (if the shape has diagonal lines) or infill as black, do not copy over the regular background pattern from the original.
Amongst the repeating pattern there is a shape of one singular color that overlaps the pattern. Create a grid of the exact same size of this shape and copy the shape and color into the grid.
Amongst the pattern there is a singular colored shape that goes across the pattern. Create a grid of the exact same size of this shape and copy it into the grid with the same color
Size of the output should be the size of the shape that is unique in the pattern of the input. Fill cells as per the unique shape's filled version in the input
Take the unique shape seen in the patterned background and replicate that. Grid size to be equal to the unique shape seen and same colour too

Well you should see one pattern that sticks out from several repeated patterns running horizontal and they are vertical from each other. Just focus on the odd pattern that is overlaying the other patterns and make your grid the size of that shape. Put that shape into your new grid using the same color the shape is in the original grid. Make sure all other squares are colored black.
The grid size will be the same as the one object that is the only object of its color. Copy this object to the grid (keep its color the same).

Within each sample, find a distinct single-colored shape that stands out from the background. Reproduce that same shape in the same color onto a grid that is not any larger than the minimum size that the shape will fully fit into without changing the size of the shape. Do not add any of the background colors into the new grid.
Ignore the background patterns and pull out and use only the shape made by a single color in one area. Nick all squares around the shape black
Copy odd shape from task with black background. Make grid as small as possible to accommodate shape.
Fit the random small shape within the pattern to an individual grid

Consider the the one distinct shape with number of sides greater then 2. 

Create a grid of the same dimentions. Copy that shape over and keep the same colours. Replace any internal colours with black.
The input grid has a shape inside of it that is out of place. The solution grid is just this shape. Copy it's dimension and color, but nothing of the original pattern that might be inside it.

you need to resize the grid to the shape that appears within the grid. there is a background shape and colour which you have to ignore. identify the shape and make the grid the same shape and colour then submit.
find the shape that doesn't fit then change the grid size to fit that shape don't include the pattern (keep colors the same
One of these don't belong. Take the oddball and match the grid size to it. Now replicate that oddball onto that grid. Color stays the same.
Look at the odd square. Make it the only thing on the grid (4x4 orange with hollow center).

Find the square in the test input grid that's a different color from the other squares. Create a grid that matches the dimensions of the different-colored square. Fill all of the grid's edge squares with the same color as the different-colored square from the test input grid.

Pick out the defriend colour shape in the grid.
Replicate just that.
Find the oddly coloured shape that overlays the other identical shapes in the grid. Resize your grid to fit this shape exactly. Draw the shape into the grid as it appears in the demonstration

There will be several squares boxes as a background and one coloured square box over the top. Identify the colour of the one square box. Resize the grid to the size of that square and duplicate it. It should just be a border.
Find the largest square.  Resize the gride to the same size as the largest square. Redraw the square, matching the color.
Make the grid size 6x6 and copy over the grey square which can be seen on the image. The square should take up the full outside edge of the grid.

Your input should show an array of squares. There should be many that are the same colour and one that is a different colour. Your submission should be that square which is a different colour. Change your submission grid size to be the same size as that shape and then copy the shape into the grid so it fits the width and height of the grid. It should be the same colour as the shape you're copying

In the input grid, there is one square that is a different color from the rest. Count the dimensions of the square. The solution grid size are the same as the dimension of the square. Then, draw a copy of the square in the new grid, using the same color, and leave the center black.

from the input grid take the object that is surrounding one of the smaller objects and reproduce the grid to this same dimension (as the object) then reproduce the surrounding object exactly, colour and dimension
This rule finds the largest square on the sample, and renders just it.  So find the largest square, make a canvas the same size, and colour the border the same.
make the grid to the same size as the odd coloured object, in the demonstration it is green and purple so you set the grid to 5 by 5 and recreate it
Change grid to 6x6. Click select, now make a gray box. The cells in the middle should all be black.
Locate the largest colored square. Resize the grid to that square size. Color the premier of the square the same color as on the original. example: If the biggest square is green, color it green.
If there is a square containing another square, copy the outermost square only, make it fit perfectly into the grid, keep the color the same.

Look at the size of the one individual colour outlined square or rectangle.  The one that there is only one of that colour.  Create a grid equal to that rectangel/square in size and colour it the same as the original.  That's all you need
Find the largest square shape on the input grid and make the grid the same size as this shape. Use the same colour as as this shape. There will only be one object. Replicate the largest square.
Resize the grid to the size of the largest square. Then draw the largest square in the grid using the same colour.

Copy from input. There will be an off colour shape highlighting another shape, copy this 'highlight' shape, make a new grid the size of this shape and click top left of it and press v to paste that shape in
You will see multiple shapes and then a ring of color. Recreate that ring as the canvas exactly and fill the inside black.

In the input there will be several rows of lines separated by empty rows. Some of these rows will be connected by shorter rows forming a box. Ignore all unconnected rows and focus only on the box. set the grid dimensions to the exact size of the square and color it the same color as the square.
Resize your grid to same size as the color box, then floodfill whole thing with same color of that box
Grid size will be full size of main object, with horizontal extents removed. Fill grid completely with color of object.

You will need to look at the grid and find the rectangle.  You will need to create a new grid that is the same size as the rectangle.  You will need to color in the new grid the same color as the rectangle.

In the middle of the lines a solid square or a rectangle is formed.  Count the number of rows and columns in this shape and create a grid equal only to this size and colour it the same as the original colour used.  You should have a grid with no blank cells.
Using the same color, isolate the solid square and resize the grid to its exact size. Fill it in with the same color. Do not include any of the lines.
get the biggest solid colored square or rectangle filled in the input without having to fill in colors and have that as the output. final out put will have no black.
In the striped grid, there will be a solid large block of a single color.  Copy only that block to the output, and keep the color the same.
Find the big block pattern in the original. Resize the new grid to be the length and width of that block pattern and fill that new grid with the color of the block from the original grid.
Find the area where the non-black squares form the biggest rectangle. The output grid is the same size and color as that rectangle. (or square-shaped area)

You should see several vertical lines but at some point there will be lines filled in where they shouldn't be. That's the shape you will be copying. You'll want to make that shape the size of your grid and you'll be assuming the shape also runs through the horizontal lines of the same color.  So you grid will always be an odd number and be at least 3 high and it will be as wide as what you see in the squares that should be black.  So basically your grid should be filled with the entire color of the horizontal lines and the shape that is 'overlapping" them. Again assume the shape overlaps the top and bottom horizontal lines as they are both the same color it's not easy to tell.

The grid should be resized to match the size and shape of the largest solid four sided shape that can be found in the input, with matching colors. A 3x5 rectangle would be a 3x5 grid with matching shape and color, or a 4x4 square.

There will be lines of a colour in the input. Within these lines there will be a block of a shape (of the same colour). Identify how big this block is and make the output grid those dimensions and then flood fill this grid with the same colour
Find the solid shape and count the squares.  Resize the grid to the same as this solid shape.
The solution is to create the shape that is seen in the input made where there are no gaps. Create the grid to replicate this and fill with the same colour (Green)
Change grid size to height 4 and width 5. Change the colour of every cell in the grid to green.
by quick decision making.

Find the objects that form perfect squares that are larger than one block. Line these square objects up horizontally next to each other in a new grid that matches the exact dimensions of these squares. There shouldn't be any left over space in the new grid.
ignore the single blocks. recreate all the shapes left to right in a grid the same size they are all together
Take the biggest shape size and put each shape of the same size into a straight line

You are looking to copy just the complete square designs. The grid needs to be changed to just hold those 3 shapes, which is a 3x9 grid. Copy the design of the squares and the colors by going from left to right in the input grid, pink then orange then yellow. They should still have the black square in the middle, exactly as in the test grid.

Ignore the sporadic coloured squares and look for the blocks of colour in the grid. change the grid size to the combined height and width of these squares and copy the coloured images exactly including any black squares in the middle.

First, identify all objects within the grid that are squares greater than 1x1 in size. Then transpose the squares to a singular row so they are flush with one another. The sequence of each square shape should reflect their appearance from left to right in the original grid. The height of the grid in the solution should reflect the height of a singular square shape, and the total width should reflect the width of a singular shape multiplied by the total number of squares. There may be square shapes within the test input grid that look irregular but ignore the jutting segments and transpose the squares minus those elements.

Identify all squares from the input that are 2x2 or larger (they can be filled or hollow). The output will consist of all of these squares placed side-by-side. As such, the height of the output should be the same height as that of a single square, and the width should be the width of the square times the number of squares. For instance, if there are 3 squares that are each 2x2, then the height of the output would be 2, and the width would be 6. Once you have the dimensions set, simply draw the squares from the input, side-by-side, in the same order they appear in the input, going from left to right (ignore how high or low they are).
There will be shapes in the input grid, Change the grid size the size of the shapes placed next to eachother and then copy the shapes into the grid size.
Reset the grid size to the size of the objects/colored blocks next to eachother (2 6x6 objects would turn to 6x12); Copy / Fill the objects into the grid size.

Use the same grid size as the size of the biggest object in the input. In your answers, at every intersection where blocks overlap, the vertical color block must overlap the horizontal color block. Keep all colors the same except the overlapping block which must have the same color as the vertical blocks

Click copy from input. This task requires you to take full shapes out of the input and then line them up side by side with the correct grid size. So select the first full Shape and press C to copy it, Reset the grid, press V on the top left square of the gird then do the same for as many shapes you see in the input, ensuring that all the shapes are perfectly touching eachother in a row, going from left to right as it is in the input image, then recolour the rest of the shapes as they are coloured in the input image, using either select or edit options depending on what the shape looks like. After that make sure the grid matches the shapes you have added, so resize the grid to perfectly match them
imagine putting the big shape next to each other, adjust grid height and width so there are no empty space. fill in with same color and shape and sequence

Eliminate any shapes that aren't squares at least as large as 3x3. Take the remaining shapes and place them in a row, next to one another, with no spaces. Adjust canvas size to match this new row of shapes. Colors stay the same.
Take note only of the square objects. From left to right, make a grid that matches all of those identically, again, going from left to right.

Change grid size to match the number of continuous shapes.
copy shapes keeping same colour
change grid to match continuous shape size from input. copy shapes and keep the same colour

Here, just take the objects that can make a regular square (all sides same length) and lay them next to each other horizontally. For eg say there are 3 squares within the input grid of 3 cells x 3 cells. The grid is then 9 cells horizontally and 3 vertically and the squares are lain right next to each other, same colour, same dimension. Now the catch here is that the square objects can be an irregular shape, but a square can be made by omitting 1 cell (do not add a cell to make a square) eg 3 cell x 3 cell square also has a cell at the bottom and to the left that bif you omit forms a perfect square.
Find the squares in the example and line them up side by side, same colour.

Find two distinct shapes on coloured backgrounds, separated by a yellow line spanning the grid.

Mentally move the shapes directly to eachother so they touch, at the yellow intersecting line, remove the yellow line and make the shapes touch.

Rezie the grid so that it accomodates the shapes exactly with no surrounding colours/background. 

The shapes should be the same colour as in the Test Input.

Focus on the 2 objects that are inside the grid. Check what the largest one is, and correspond this to the height/width of your grid. To find the height (or width if the previous step gave you the height), add the number of squares of the widest point of your shapes combined to give you this grid size. Then draw those focal objects inside the grid

Draw on the shapes either side of the dividing yellow line, in a grid that is big as the two shapes drawn next to each other (i.e. touching), but no bigger. Meaning at the largest extent of the shape is at the edge of the grid. The shapes must be in the same orientation as the original

There should be two clear shapes/objects separated by a yellow line. If the objects are separated by right and left sides, your goal is to put them right next to each other. If they are separated by top and bottom, your goal is to put the top object right above the bottom object. There should be no spaces inbetween them. Make the grid size the same size as exactly how much space is needed when combining them (ex: height of the top object + the height of the bottom object = the height of the grid. width of the bottom object = the width of the widest object). The background should be kept black. Copy the objects over and submit.

Keep the input objects the same size but remove any blocks between them. Erase the line of blocks that is separating the two objects and draw the objects touching each other but keep them the same size as the original.

If the yellow bar is vertical, the grid is equal to the width of the shape within the other color to the left of the bar plus the shape within the other color to the right of the bar. If the yellow bar is horizontal, the grid height is equal to the height of the shape within the other color above the yellow bar plus that of the shape within the other color below the yellow bar. The height or width of the grid is the same as the height or width of the original height or width of those corresponding shapes. Now put those two shapes into the grid right next to each other in the same up/down or left/right positions. Copy the color and shape for each.

There are two outlines of colored tiles surrounded by another color on either side of the yellow line. Make a grid the size of the width and height of those combined, and transfer them over in the orientation that they were in, either vertically or horizontally, directly near each other with all the other tiles empty.
Put both of the shapes across the line from each other together. Touching. Get rid of the line. Make the grid only fit the shapes.

Two objects of similar size (same height and width, can look different though like a square and diamond) will have an interrupting object between them. That needs removed and the two remaining objects will have to be touching in a new grid that exactly matches the height and width of the new objects. For example, two 4x4 squares have a line between them with one on top and one between, remove the line, reposition the squares to they fit exactly on each other, and resize the grid to fit the new shape. Keep the colors the same as they were.

Ensure the shapes above and below or to the left and right are in line with each other. Now remove the dividing line and move the two shapes next to each other. The dimensions are those of the two shapes next to each other and you colour in the shapes next to each other as described.
Take the two shapes shown divided by the line and copy them so there is no border or spaces between the shapes (they are sandwiched together). Resize the grid to just fit the two shapes.
remove the space and middle shape line. fill in height and width enough to fit remaining shape. keep same color and shape.
Remove the item that looks like a barrier between the two pictures and align the two pictures so they are centered.
Resize the grid so that it's the same size as the two main shapes. Remove the extra rows in the grid so that the two shapes are touching. Keep the design of each original shape the same.

You will have two colored objects divided by a row or rows of another color.  Resize the grid so that it is the same size as the two large objects side by side or one atop the other.  Your new grid should be these two objects only in the same colors.

Identify the two objects separated by a divider. The grid size should be the minimal size necessary to contain them both (without divider) in the same orientation (i.e. on top of each other, or side-by-side). Replicate the objects in this space with their same relative orientation, now touching.
Connect the two shapes either side of the dividing line centering them in a new grid such that no blank columns exist beyond the size of the largest shape.

The rule is that the main shapes should be moved so that they are adjacent to each ither, and the grid resized so that it s height and width mathc that of the shapes.  You should resize the grid to 4 x 8, and then use the edit function to recreate the shapes side by side, with the shapes maintaining their right and left or upa nd down orientation to each other.
Take out the colored line between shapes, merge them and then resize the grid to the size of the two shapes merged.
The grid size will change to match the size of the two objects. The dividing line should be removed, but it will notify whether the combination will be horizontal or vertical.
Remove the divider object from the grid.  Create a grid the size of the two remaining object when moved to touch each other. Copy the two objects so they touch and keeping the same colors.
remove the line and join the 2 shapes together. The grid size is only the 2 shapes joined together with no blacks square around the edges. Colours will be the same
Find any polygons and delete everything except for the interiors of them, such that a rectangle encompassing every light colored interior square remains.

the old grid should have design(s).  the perimeter of the design(s) should be black.  
the new shape should be a copy of the center of the designs in the old grid.  if there are more than one design, the centers of the designs should be side-by-side in the same left to right order as the old grid.  the dimensions of the new grid are the minimum size needed to completely fit the centers of the shapes in the old grid.
Create a grid the size of the colored area(s) within the object(s)s of input grid.  The new grid should be filled with the same color and have the same shape.

Find the shapes INSIDE the shapes in the demonstration grid. Redraw your grid to the height and width of the inside shapes. Draw these shapes onto the grid left to right as they appear on the demonstraion grid.
adjust the grid to the exact dimensions of the shape in surrounded by black blocks and then duplicate that shape in the resized grid keeping the colour the same

The rule is that you are going to only copy the CENTERS of the black shapes, in order from left to right. Resize the grid to 2Hx4W. Then "select" all 8 boxes in the grid and choose the dark blue color at the bottom. Then click flood fill and click any square in the grid to make them all dark blue.
Align the inner shapes matching the colors of the example. Resize the grid to fit these inner shapes lined up in order and fill in with the coloring tools.
Make a grid the same size as the centre of the shape and colour this the same as the centre
put the shapes inside the black squares in a horizontal line connected to each other with as little grid space as you can manage
Take the inside sections of each shape and make the grid the size of these next to each other. Keep the same colour.
I USED THE INPUT GRID OF THE SIZE.

Look at the original grid. The shapes created by the different colors, your task is to copy these down. If there is more than one, please create the grid such that they are next to eachother in the same order they are in the original. For example, if there was a square with a single red block in the middle you would submit a 1x1 grid filled in red.

There will be one or more shapes in black, on a coloured background. If the shape is a square or rectangle, count the cells on the inside and set your grid to that size. Flood it with the colour of the background. If the shape is irregular (not made of straight lines) count the cells inside and also the black cells that make it a square or rectangle. Copy this shape onto your grid.

Change the grid size to 6x3. Based on the task demonstration there is a pattern. The smallest object inside of the grid is copied by its actual size in the answer. In your answer, fill in the grid with the same color and size of the small object. After, change the two corner squares to black.

Create a grid with the dimensions of the shape inside the black outline from the original and fill in the shape with the same color. If ther is more than one black outline with a shape inside, draw them side by side.

Once again, adjust the width and height of the grid that we have until it matches the input. Copy the design of the input onto the grid.

Identify the "centres"/"middles" of the shapes/designs of the input. Now, adjust the width and height of the grid again until it matches the height and width of the "centres"/"middles".

Slowly and carefully, by using the same colour, re-create the design of the "centres"/"middles".
Pick the smaller interior design that has another color in it. Resize grid to match. Copy color of main interior design but change the smaller design to black.

Find the group of squares with the same color (not black) inside the group of squares with a different color. Create a grid that has the same height and width as the inside group. Using the group's color, color in the squares that correspond to the group. Leave all other squares black.
reset the grid to focus on the extent of the smaller coloured shape shown on the example and then replicate it using a single colour with the central blocks made black
out of the 3 coloured cells used, only the inside patterns were used, however the most inner pattern were blacked out.

In the input, there should be a shape enveloped by another shape. Imagine cutting this shape out. This is the size of the grid you should use, and any empty spaces that aren't the same colour as the enveloped shape should be left black.

Look at the shape within the coloured rectangle. Translate that using the same colour, but within that shape, fill the cells with black, (where contrasting colour used to be). Then resize the grid to fit only this shape.

The input will have a shape laid over (and within) another shape. Make your output the size and color of this inner shape. Then, make any "holes", through which the color of the outer shape was visible in the input, black in the output.

You're going to make the grid 4Hx5W. You're going to select the entire grid and fill it with the bright blue color by clicking 'fill'. Then you are going to select the bottom left and right corner and press the black color to turn them black. You will also select the top 3 center blocks to make black as well using the same technique. Then you will make the block in the 4th row down and center black as well by selecting it and then clicking on the black color.

Find the inner shape that's a different colour.  Resize your grid size to the same size as this (using black squares around this if the shape isn't a square or rectangle).  Replace any colours in the middle of this shape to be black, but the shape will be the same colour.
Use a 4 height by 5 width grid. Copy the position and colour of the blue object in the original. The colour remains unchanged (blue). Leave the remaining squares black.
Resize your grid to same height and width as the most inner shape, then keep the inner shape only, every other cell would be black
Change the grid size to the the size of the object with the smallest count of coloured cubes and recreate that object exactly

Identify unique feature in the input (of a different color). Your grid dimensions in your solution will match the longest (height) and widest (width) parts of this unique feature - do not copy original dimensions of input. Recreate this unique shape, keeping only the original color of the unique shape, but not keeping the color of the larger input grid. If the unique shape encloses a part of the larger object in the input grid, that piece is now only made of black grid squares. Your solution only reflects the dimensions and color of that unique shape.

The rule is to focus on just the object in the middle of the grid (in this case the light blue object) and make the outside of it a rectangular shape with all other squares black. To do so, you will make a grid that is 5Hx7W. Then you will copy the original light blue object into that grid, starting on left in the middle square. The column next to that should have 3 light blue squares directly connected to the leftmost light blue square, the next column should have 5 light blue squares, and then there should be 4 light blue squares in a row connected to that column. Leave the black squares in the grid alone.

Copy the shape that is contained within the shape that is not the black background colour. The grid is the sized to the extent of the copied shaped. Any other colour that is not the colour of the copied object is replace with the black background object
